refresh_policy.rationale: Chase requires OTP and short-lived browser sessions; never run a bank refresh in the background.

streams.name: accounts
streams.description: Chase retail accounts (checking, savings, credit cards). Hybrid-sourced: identity + account type come from the QFX ACCTINFO response; friendly name, open date, and tier come from chase.com dashboard scrape.

display.label: Your Chase accounts
display.detail: Account names, types, last-four, current balance, and status. Credit card accounts additionally surface available credit and statement balance.

id.description: Stable connector-scoped identifier. Currently a hash of (account_type, account_number); will stabilize on Chase's internal account id once we scrape it from the dashboard DOM.

balance_cents.description: Ledger balance as of statement_as_of_date (QFX LEDGERBAL).

credit_limit_cents.description: Credit cards only. Null for checking/savings.

available_credit_cents.description: Credit cards only. Null for checking/savings.

statement_balance_cents.description: Credit cards only. Balance on most recent statement; null for checking/savings.

streams.name: transactions
streams.description: Chase transactions per account, parsed from QFX (Quicken Web Connect) downloads. QFX is Chase's canonical transaction-export format: per-request date window is ~90 days, so the connector walks backward in 90-day chunks until QFX returns empty. Primary key is (account_id, fitid) — Chase's QFX FITID is a stable unique transaction identifier, so dedup across overlapping date windows collapses naturally. Amounts are in cents (integer, signed).

display.label: Your Chase transactions
display.detail: Date, signed amount in cents, cleaned payee name, original memo, Chase-assigned transaction type (DEBIT/CREDIT/CHECK/XFER/PAYMENT/FEE/etc.), check number, and FITID (Chase's stable transaction identifier). Pending transactions are not included — QFX delivers posted transactions only.

id.description: ${account_id}|${fitid}. Chase's FITID is stable across re-exports.

fitid.description: Chase's internal Financial Institution Transaction ID. Verbatim from QFX STMTTRN.

amount.description: Signed amount in cents. Negative for debits, positive for credits.

currency.description: Always 'USD' for Chase retail accounts. QFX CURDEF.

type.description: QFX TRNTYPE: CREDIT | DEBIT | CHECK | XFER | PAYMENT | DEP | DIRECTDEP | DIRECTDEBIT | ATM | POS | FEE | SRVCHG | INT | DIV | CASH | OTHER.

name.description: Payee or merchant name. QFX NAME.

memo.description: QFX MEMO. Often contains merchant city for CC transactions, or the raw bank descriptor for checking.

reference_number.description: QFX REFNUM. Chase uses this for Zelle and wire transfers.

source.description: Always 'qfx_download_<YYYY-MM-DD>_to_<YYYY-MM-DD>'. Provenance for audit and re-extraction.

streams.name: current_activity
streams.description: Chase UI-visible account activity at scrape time. This mutable freshness stream can include pending rows and recently posted current-cycle rows from the signed-in Chase activity UI. It is not the settled QFX ledger; pending rows may change, disappear, or later post under a different QFX FITID.

display.label: Your current Chase activity
display.detail: Pending and current-cycle activity visible in Chase's account UI. Use this for freshness, not reconciliation. Posted ledger identity remains in the QFX-only transactions stream.

id.description: ${account_id}|${ui_transaction_id} when Chase exposes a UI id; otherwise ${account_id}|fallback:${hash(status|activity_date|posted_date|amount|description)}. Fallback keys do not promise pending-to-posted continuity.

amount.description: Signed amount in cents. Negative for visible debits, positive for visible credits.

currency.description: Always 'USD' for Chase retail account UI rows in this connector.

ui_transaction_id.description: Source-provided Chase UI transaction/activity id when present in DOM; null when the connector used the deterministic fallback key.

streams.name: statements
streams.description: Monthly statement PDFs per account from Chase's Statements & Documents page. Each record carries the document's title, delivery date, account reference, and — when the PDF is hydrated on-device — a content-addressed path + sha256. PDFs themselves live on the owner's disk under ~/.pdpp/chase-statements/<account>/<YYYY-MM>-<sha8>.pdf rather than in the RS, keeping blob storage out of SQLite per `design-notes/blob-hydration-open-question.md`.

display.label: Your Chase statements
display.detail: PDF monthly statements across all Chase accounts. Metadata is always captured (title, delivery date, account reference); PDF bytes are downloaded and stored locally with a content hash when blob hydration runs. The same hash reappears across runs so downloads are idempotent.

account_id.description: FK to accounts.id. Resolved by matching the statement row's account_reference back to the accounts stream. Null when neither last-four nor name substring match succeed.

title.description: Row title from the Statements & Documents table, e.g. `Sapphire Preferred (...9241)`.

document_url.description: file:// URI for the stored PDF on disk. Populated only after blob hydration; null on index-only scrapes.

pdf_path.description: Absolute filesystem path of the downloaded PDF under ~/.pdpp/chase-statements/. Null until hydrated.

pdf_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the downloaded PDF bytes.

streams.name: balances
streams.description: Point-in-time balance snapshots per account. One record per QFX response per account. Useful for reconstructing historical balances between statement dates.

display.label: Your Chase balance snapshots
display.detail: Ledger and available balance per account, timestamped to the QFX response's balance-as-of date. Each run produces one snapshot per account; snapshots accumulate rather than replace, preserving the history.

as_of.description: QFX DTASOF — when Chase measured the balance.
